shared_properties: The layer spans the full screen and remains non-interactive like world_far_tree_silhouettes_1280x720.
The center opening stays aligned with the gameplay corridor so obstacles remain easy to read.
The silhouette remains static so scrolling motion comes from code-side parallax only.
differences: This variant darkens the tree masses and tightens the side framing to increase motion pressure.
The central opening is slightly narrower than world_far_tree_silhouettes_1280x720 so the scene feels more enclosed.
Trunk forms are chunkier to hold up under the faster 0.7x parallax rate.

context.usage: Faster parallax tree band that tightens the corridor and increases depth at gameplay speed.

shape.silhouette: Closer tree trunks and branches framing the sides with a sharp central opening for the fall lane.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 1280x720px with no opaque pixels entering the central 240px fall lane.
Near trunks and branches use visibly darker values than the far layer so depth ordering reads instantly.
The frame edge detail stays chunky enough to avoid shimmer under 60 FPS scrolling.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the sepia autumn atmosphere, tree silhouettes, and warm color layering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle parallax entity with a stronger movement factor tied to camera descent.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/world_near_tree_silhouettes_1280x720.png during boot and apply a 0.7x vertical parallax factor during gameplay.
runtime_notes: Scroll the texture at 0.7x of the player fall speed so the near band adds urgency without covering the lane center.
Keep the central opening aligned with the obstacle corridor and the player anchor position.
Use darker values than world_far_tree_silhouettes_1280x720 so the near layer reads as a stronger framing element.
